title: Masala Egg & Black Bean Bowl
tagline: Indian-spiced scrambled eggs over black beans with spinach. High-protein, 20-min meal.
instructions: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds until they pop. Add onion, cook 4 min until golden. Add garlic, ginger, tomatoes, turmeric, coriander — cook 3 min. Add black beans, warm through 2 min. Push to side; add beaten eggs, scramble slowly into the masala. Fold in spinach until just wilted. Divide into 2 bowls. Top with garam masala and cilantro.

title: Spiced Lentil Dal with Cauliflower
tagline: Batch-cook Sunday staple. Freezes well. Turmeric + cumin base for PCOS-friendly blood sugar control.
instructions: Rinse lentils. Heat ghee in large pot; add cumin seeds until they pop. Add onion, cook 5 min until golden. Add garlic and ginger, cook 2 min. Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander — cook 3 min. Add lentils and stock/water. Bring to boil, reduce, simmer 15 min. Add cauliflower florets, simmer 10 more min until lentils dissolve and cauliflower is tender. Stir in spinach, garam masala, lemon juice. Adjust salt. Makes 4 large portions — refrigerates 5 days, freezes 3 months.

title: Tofu Miso Stir-Fry with Broccoli
tagline: Japanese-style umami tofu with broccoli and edamame. 15-minute weeknight dinner.
ingredients: 400g (1 block) — Extra-firm tofu, pressed and cubed
200g (2 cups) — Shiitake or cremini mushrooms, sliced
instructions: Press tofu 15 min then cube into 2cm pieces. Whisk miso, tamari, rice vinegar, 2 tbsp water into sauce. Heat olive oil in wok or large pan over high heat. Add tofu, cook undisturbed 3 min per side until golden. Remove. Add mushrooms, cook 3 min. Add broccoli, edamame, garlic, ginger — stir fry 4 min. Return tofu. Pour sauce over, toss 1 min. Drizzle sesame oil, top with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve as-is (no rice — slow carb).

title: Paneer & Chickpea Tikka Bowl
tagline: Pan-charred paneer cubes with spiced chickpeas and roasted veg. No naan, no rice — all protein.
ingredients: Fresh cilantro + lime wedge to serve
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes in yogurt + garam masala + lemon juice for 15 min (or overnight). Heat olive oil in oven-proof pan; roast peppers and zucchini at 400°F 15 min. Meanwhile, heat ghee in pan, add cumin seeds, onion, garlic — cook 4 min. Add chickpeas, turmeric, paprika, chili — stir fry 5 min until chickpeas are crispy. In separate pan, cook marinated paneer on high heat 2-3 min per side until charred. Combine veg, chickpeas, paneer in bowl. Top with fresh cilantro and lime.

title: Egg & Lentil Power Breakfast
tagline: Hard-boiled eggs over warm spiced lentils. Batch the lentils Sunday — assembly takes 3 minutes.
ingredients: 300g (1½ cups cooked) — Green or black lentils
instructions: Warm lentils in a pan with a splash of water. In separate small pan, heat ghee, add onion + garlic + cumin + turmeric — cook 3 min until golden. Pour tadka (tempered spice oil) over lentils. Add ACV, stir. Wilt spinach in same pan 1 min. Plate: spinach base, lentils on top, halved eggs alongside. Season with salt, pepper, chili flakes. Takes 5 min if lentils are pre-cooked from Sunday batch.

title: Black Bean & Roasted Veg Salad with Tahini
tagline: Big, filling lunch that travels well. Pack dressing separately. No wilting by noon.
ingredients: 60g (2 big handfuls) — Baby spinach or arugula
Salt, cumin, chili flakes to taste
instructions: Roast broccoli and bell pepper at 400°F 20 min with olive oil and salt. Cool slightly. Whisk tahini, lemon, garlic, water, ACV, cumin into dressing — add water until pourable. In large bowl: spinach base, black beans, roasted veg, tomatoes, cucumber, red onion. Pack dressing separately if bringing to work. Top with pumpkin seeds and drizzle dressing at eating time.

title: Spiced Turkey & Lentil Keema
tagline: When you want meat. Ground turkey stretched with green lentils — cheaper, higher fiber, more filling than pure meat.
ingredients: Fresh cilantro, lemon juice to finish
instructions: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds. Add onion, cook 5 min until golden. Add garlic, ginger — 2 min. Add bell pepper, cook 3 min. Add turkey, break up and cook through 5-7 min. Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, chili — stir well. Fold in cooked lentils and peas. Simmer 10 min until everything melds. Finish with garam masala, lemon juice, cilantro. Serve solo or over cauliflower rice. Makes 4 servings — fridge 4 days.
